annotations.label: Magma

anchor.snippet: A magma is a set M with an operation

annotations.note: Mathlib has no separate `Magma` class; the typeclass `Mul` (a type with a binary operation `* : α → α → α`) is the formalization of a magma.

annotations.label: Closure property

anchor.snippet: is known as the magma or closure property

annotations.note: Closure is implicit in the type `Mul α` whose operation has type `α → α → α`.

annotations.label: Partial magma / partial groupoid

anchor.snippet: is called a partial magma

annotations.note: No notion of partial magma (partial binary operation) was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Morphism of magmas

anchor.snippet: A morphism of magmas is a function

annotations.note: `MulHom M N` (notation `M →ₙ* N`) is exactly a function preserving the binary operation between two `Mul`-types.

annotations.label: Logarithm as a magma morphism

anchor.snippet: a logarithm f is a morphism of the magma

annotations.note: `Real.log_mul` proves `log (x*y) = log x + log y`, but `log` is not bundled as an explicit `MulHom`/`AddHom` between positive reals and reals.

annotations.label: Catalan number C_2 = 2

anchor.snippet: which is just the statement that

annotations.note: `catalan_two : catalan 2 = 2` is stated and proved in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Catalan number C_3 = 5

anchor.snippet: Less trivially

annotations.note: `catalan_three : catalan 3 = 5` is stated and proved in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Number of magmas on an n-element set

anchor.snippet: there are n n 2 magmas associated with that set

annotations.note: No enumeration result counting magma structures (i.e. arbitrary binary operations) on a finite set as `n^(n^2)` was found.

annotations.label: Free magma

anchor.snippet: A free magma M X on a set X is the

annotations.note: `FreeMagma α` is the inductive type of formal non-associative products of elements of `α`.

annotations.label: Universal property of the free magma

anchor.snippet: A free magma has the universal property

annotations.note: `FreeMagma.lift : (α → β) ≃ (FreeMagma α →ₙ* β)` packages the universal property as an explicit equivalence.

annotations.label: Quasigroup

anchor.snippet: Quasigroup : A magma where division is always possible

annotations.note: No `Quasigroup` class formalizing unique left/right divisibility was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Loop

anchor.snippet: Loop : A quasigroup with an identity element

annotations.note: No `Loop` (quasigroup with identity) algebraic class was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Semigroup

anchor.snippet: Semigroup : A magma where the operation is associative

annotations.note: `Semigroup` extends `Mul` with associativity `mul_assoc`.

annotations.label: Monoid

anchor.snippet: Monoid : A semigroup with an identity element

annotations.note: `Monoid` extends `Semigroup` and `MulOneClass`, i.e. a semigroup with an identity element.

annotations.label: Group

anchor.snippet: Group : A magma with inverse, associativity, and an identity element

annotations.note: `Group` is defined as a monoid in which every element has an inverse.

annotations.label: Divisibility/invertibility imply cancellation

anchor.snippet: each of divisibility and invertibility imply the cancellation property

annotations.note: Mathlib proves invertibility implies cancellation via `Group.toCancelMonoid`, but the more general statement about divisibility/quasigroups giving cancellation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Commutative magma

anchor.snippet: Commutative magma : A magma with commutativity

annotations.note: `CommMagma` extends `Mul` with `mul_comm`.

annotations.label: Commutative monoid

anchor.snippet: Commutative monoid : A monoid with commutativity

annotations.note: `CommMonoid` extends `Monoid` and `CommSemigroup`.

annotations.label: Abelian group

anchor.snippet: Abelian group : A group with commutativity

annotations.note: `CommGroup` extends `Group` and `CommMonoid`.

annotations.label: Medial magma

anchor.snippet: If it satisfies the identity xy • uz

annotations.note: The medial identity `(xy)(uz) = (xu)(yz)` is not formalized as a typeclass in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Left semimedial

anchor.snippet: If it satisfies the identity xx • yz ≡ xy • xz

annotations.note: No formalization of left semimediality was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Right semimedial

anchor.snippet: If it satisfies the identity yz • xx ≡ yx • zx

annotations.note: No formalization of right semimediality was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Semimedial

anchor.snippet: If it is both left and right semimedial

annotations.note: No formalization of semimediality (both sides) was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Left distributive

anchor.snippet: If it satisfies the identity x • yz ≡ xy • xz

annotations.note: Self-distributivity `x*(y*z) = (x*y)*(x*z)` is not formalized; Mathlib's `LeftDistribClass` is the two-operation ring axiom.

annotations.label: Right distributive

anchor.snippet: If it satisfies the identity yz • x ≡ yx • zx

annotations.note: Self-right-distributivity is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Autodistributive

anchor.snippet: If it is both left and right distributive

annotations.note: No formalization of autodistributive magmas was found.

annotations.label: Commutative

anchor.snippet: If it satisfies the identity xy ≡ yx

annotations.note: `CommMagma` captures a magma satisfying `xy = yx`.

annotations.label: Idempotent

anchor.snippet: If it satisfies the identity xx ≡ x

annotations.note: Mathlib has `IsIdempotentElem` for individual elements but no typeclass asserting every element of a magma is idempotent.

annotations.label: Unipotent

anchor.snippet: If it satisfies the identity xx ≡ yy

annotations.note: No formalization of unipotent magmas was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Zeropotent

anchor.snippet: If it satisfies the identities xx • y ≡ xx ≡ y • xx

annotations.note: No formalization of zeropotent magmas was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Alternative

anchor.snippet: If it satisfies the identities xx • y ≡ x • xy and x • yy ≡ xy • y

annotations.note: No formalization of alternative magmas (left/right alternative laws) was found; `Alternative` in Mathlib refers to a control-flow typeclass.

annotations.label: Power-associative

anchor.snippet: If the submagma generated by any element is associative

annotations.note: No formalization of power-associative magmas was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Flexible

anchor.snippet: if xy • x ≡ x • yx

annotations.note: No formalization of the flexible identity `(xy)x = x(yx)` was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Associative (semigroup)

anchor.snippet: If it satisfies the identity x • yz ≡ xy • z , called a semigroup

annotations.note: `Semigroup` is exactly a magma whose multiplication satisfies `mul_assoc`.

annotations.label: Left-unar

anchor.snippet: If it satisfies the identity xy ≡ xz

annotations.note: No formalization of left-unar magmas was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Right-unar

anchor.snippet: If it satisfies the identity yx ≡ zx

annotations.note: No formalization of right-unar magmas was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Constant magma

anchor.snippet: If it satisfies the identity xy ≡ uv

annotations.note: No formalization of constant magmas was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unital

anchor.snippet: If it has an identity element

annotations.note: `MulOneClass` is a `Mul` together with an identity element `1` satisfying `one_mul` and `mul_one`.

annotations.label: Left-cancellative

anchor.snippet: relation xy = xz implies y = z

annotations.note: `IsLeftCancelMul G` asserts every element of `G` is left-regular, equivalent to left cancellation.

annotations.label: Right-cancellative

anchor.snippet: relation yx = zx implies y = z

annotations.note: `IsRightCancelMul G` asserts every element is right-regular, equivalent to right cancellation.

annotations.label: Cancellative

anchor.snippet: If it is both right-cancellative and left-cancellative

annotations.note: `IsCancelMul` extends both `IsLeftCancelMul` and `IsRightCancelMul`.

annotations.label: Left-zero semigroup

anchor.snippet: If it is a semigroup and it satisfies the identity xy ≡ x

annotations.note: No formalization of left-zero semigroups was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Right-zero semigroup

anchor.snippet: If it is a semigroup and it satisfies the identity yx ≡ x

annotations.note: No formalization of right-zero semigroups was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Triple-medial

anchor.snippet: If any triple of (not necessarily distinct) elements generates a medial submagma

annotations.note: No formalization of triple-medial magmas was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Image of a medial cancellation magma

anchor.snippet: If it is a homomorphic image of a medial cancellation magma

annotations.note: No formalization of this notion was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Squag/identity xy • yz ≡ y

anchor.snippet: If it satisfies the identity xy • yz ≡ y

annotations.note: No formalization of squags (or this identity as a typeclass) was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Category of magmas Mag

anchor.snippet: The category of magmas, denoted Mag

annotations.note: `MagmaCat` is the bundled category of magmas with `MulHom` morphisms.

annotations.label: Mag is a complete category

anchor.snippet: because Mag is algebraic , it is a complete category

annotations.note: No `HasLimits MagmaCat` instance or completeness theorem for `MagmaCat` was found.

annotations.label: Extension of injective endomorphism to automorphism

anchor.snippet: an injective endomorphism can be extended to an automorphism of a magma extension

annotations.note: No theorem extending an injective magma endomorphism to an automorphism of an extension was found in Mathlib.
